title: Nuclear materials, facilities, equipment + miscellaneous
text: EAR Category 0: Nuclear materials, facilities, and equipment + miscellaneous items. Most items in this category are also subject to specific Nuclear Regulatory Commission jurisdiction.

title: Electronics
text: EAR Category 3: Electronics design, development, and production. Includes integrated circuits, microelectronics, sensors, lasers, electronic test equipment.

title: Computers
text: EAR Category 4: High-performance computers, software, and technology. ECCN 4A003 covers digital computers with adjusted peak performance above specified thresholds; ECCN 4D001 covers software for the development of computers; ECCN 4E001 covers technology.

title: Telecommunications + Information Security
text: Category 5 Part 1: Telecommunications. Part 2: Information Security — ECCN 5A002 (cryptographic items), 5D002 (software), 5E002 (technology). Many encryption items qualify for ENC license exception or TSU exception subject to filing.

title: Aerospace + Propulsion
text: EAR Category 9: Spacecraft, satellites, rocket systems, propulsion. Note: Some space-related items moved between EAR and ITAR via Export Control Reform (2014).

title: EAR99
text: Items subject to the EAR but not specifically listed on the CCL. EAR99 items can generally be exported without a license to most destinations, BUT still require: (a) destination not embargoed; (b) end-user not on Entity List / SDN / MEU; (c) end-use not prohibited (e.g. WMD).

title: Firearms, Close Assault Weapons, Combat Shotguns
text: USML Category I: Firearms < .50 caliber, fully automatic firearms, etc. Articles subject to ITAR are under DDTC jurisdiction, not BIS-EAR. Manufacturers and exporters of USML items must register with DDTC.

title: Launch Vehicles, Guided Missiles, Ballistic Missiles, Rockets, Torpedoes, Bombs and Mines
text: USML Category IV: includes complete launch vehicles, missiles, propulsion systems, guidance / control systems, warheads. Subject to broad ITAR controls + MTCR Category I.

title: Military Electronics
text: USML Category XI: Military-specific electronics — radar, signals intelligence, electronic warfare, military encryption, secure communications, infrared sensors with military specs.

title: Spacecraft and Related Articles
text: USML Category XV: Specific spacecraft items remaining under ITAR control post-Export Control Reform (most commercial communications satellites transitioned to EAR).

title: ITAR technical data
text: ITAR technical data = information required for the design, development, production, manufacture, assembly, operation, repair, testing, maintenance, or modification of defense articles. Includes blueprints, manuals, software documentation, training materials. Release to a foreign national IN THE US = a 'deemed export' requiring authorization.

title: Cuban Assets Control Regulations
text: Comprehensive embargo. General licenses + specific licenses available for limited categories (humanitarian, journalism, telecom, agricultural sales).

title: Iranian Transactions and Sanctions Regulations
text: Comprehensive embargo. Limited general licenses for humanitarian goods, certain personal communications, some software. Secondary sanctions on non-US persons doing significant transactions.

title: Russia sanctions
text: Multiple Executive Orders + sectoral sanctions (financial, energy, defense). Crimea / DPR / LPR specific embargoes. SSI (Sectoral Sanctions Identifications) lists. Constantly evolving in response to events.

title: North Korea Sanctions
text: Comprehensive embargo + UN Security Council sanctions implementation. Virtually all transactions prohibited absent specific authorization.

title: BIS Entity List
text: Entities on the Entity List require a license for exports / re-exports / transfers of items subject to EAR. License application presumption is generally denial. Used to restrict items going to entities engaged in proliferation, supporting terrorism, evading sanctions, etc.

title: Military End User (MEU) restrictions
text: Restrictions on items destined for military end users in: Belarus, Burma, Cambodia, China, Russia, Venezuela, Nicaragua. License required for items in the ECCN list at Supplement 2 to Part 744.

title: BIS Red Flag Indicators (29-flag list)
text: BIS guidance lists ~29 red-flag indicators: customer reluctance to provide end-use/end-user info; cash payment for high-value items; unusual shipping route via transshipment country; customer requests EAR99 classification without justification; product capabilities don't match stated end-use; customer is a freight forwarder for an unknown ultimate consignee; etc.

title: Optical, photographic, measuring, medical instruments
text: HTSUS Chapter 90: optical / photographic / cinematographic / measuring / checking / precision / medical / surgical instruments. Common HTS for AI hardware (specifically: optical instruments + measuring equipment subheadings).

title: Machinery + mechanical appliances
text: HTSUS Chapter 84: very broad — covers most industrial machinery. AI servers + GPUs often classified here (8471 = automatic data processing machines).
